what_it_does: Records an image attachment in the design-assets channel by storing YAML frontmatter that maps an attachment id to an image asset target and a timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
standouts: The target URI uses an image:// scheme.
The referenced filename contains spaces and parentheses: 'image (17)1'.
It is a minimal ledger-style attachment record.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References an asset with a protocol-prefixed URI.
concerns: The target string contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may complicate URI parsing.
No checksum, MIME type, size, or uploader metadata is recorded.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry for a design-assets image with a messy, parenthesized filename.
questions: How is the image:// URI resolved?
Who or what process created this attachment?
Is there an associated channel message or asset record?
What is the actual file extension of the image?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an image attachment, and the file does exactly that through metadata.
intention_signals: Catalog a specific image attachment in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced by id and target later.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme and a target filename with spaces/parentheses are slightly unusual; otherwise the record is a conventional metadata stub.
contribution: Provides a concrete attachment record that links an attachment id to a design-assets image target and creation timestamp, which a fused system could retain for asset lineage.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-c4d446c5 in the design-assets channel references image target image://design-assets/icons/image (17)1 and was added on 2026-06-22T12:26:25.005915+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: Record a design-assets image attachment for lookup and provenance tracking.
